spec.description:
Number of CQL batches command, each batched command is counted once.

Is generally used to group mutations for a single partition and do not suffer from the performance penalty imposed by logged batches, but there is no atomicity guarantee for multi-partition updates.
spec.title: CQL Unlogged Batches by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of CQL command batched. Each batch would add the number of commands inside the batch
spec.title: CQL Command In Batches by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of CQL row reads
spec.title: CQL Row Reads by [[by]]

spec.description: Number of reads using secondary indexes
spec.title: Secondary indexes Reads by [[by]]

spec.description: Requests by Consistency Level
spec.title: Requests by Consistency Level by [[by]]

options.content:
CQL System tables
The following information is based on Scylla Plugin configuration, check the documentation for more details on how to enable it.

spec.queryText: select address, port, shard_id, connection_stage, client_type, ssl_enabled, username, driver_name, driver_version, protocol_version from system.clients
select keyspace_name, table_name,partition_key, clustering_key, row_size,compaction_time from system.large_rows
select keyspace_name, table_name,partition_key, clustering_key, column_name,cell_size, collection_elements, compaction_time from system.large_cells
select keyspace_name, table_name, partition_key, partition_size, compaction_time, rows, dead_rows, range_tombstones from system.large_partitions

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_cql_inserts{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) - sum(rate(scylla_cql_inserts_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

spec.description:
Number of CQL INSERT commands generated by the user

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) - sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

spec.description: Number of CQL SELECT commands generated by the user
spec.title: CQL Reads by [[by]]

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(cql:non_system_prepared1m)/ (sum(cql:all_shardrate1m{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) - sum(cql:all_system_shardrate1m{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}))) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
All of the requests should be prepared

Prepared statements remove the overhead of parsing the query every time and allow optimal routing of requests from client to server
spec.title: CQL Non-Prepared Statements

spec.description: All of the requests should be prepared
spec.title: CQL Non-Prepared Statements

spec.expr: 100 * ((sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))-sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries_per_ks{ks="system",instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])))/sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
All requests should be paged

Non Paged request sources:
- Client modifying the fetch size

Non Paged requests require reading all the results and returning them in a single request.
spec.title: Non-Paged CQL Reads

spec.expr: sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])-sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries_per_ks{ks="system",instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])

spec.description: Non-Paged requests require reading all the results and returning them in a single request
spec.title: Non-Paged CQL Reads

spec.expr: clamp_max(100*((sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)) + (sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_writes_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)))/((sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) + 1) or vector(1)),100) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
All of the requests should be Token Aware

Non Token Aware requests sources:
* Non-Prepared Stamements
* Client not using a Token Aware load balancing policy

TokenAware requests are sent to a Scylla node that is also a replica. Token Un-Aware requests require extra hop and additional processing.

Note that the metric shows incorrect values when batches are used.
spec.title: Non-Token Aware

spec.expr: (sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on() sum(scylla_reactor_utilization*0) by([[by]]))

(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_writes_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on() sum(scylla_reactor_utilization*0) by([[by]]))

spec.description:
Requests that are not token aware indicates that requests are not routed to the right node, which require extra hop and additional processing.

Note that the metric shows incorrect values when batches are used.
spec.title: Non-Token Aware Queries

spec.expr: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_reverse_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
Reversed CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side

Sources: CQL Read requests with ORDER BY that is different from the "CLUSTERING ORDER BY" of the table
Alternatives:

* Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
spec.title: Reversed CQL Reads

spec.description: Reversed CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side and should be avoided
spec.title: Reversed CQL Reads

spec.expr: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_read_requests{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads, the percentage of read requests with 'ALLOW FILTERING'

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side

Sources: CQL Read requests with "ALLOW FILTERING"

ALLOW FILTERING should be used when large parts of the filtered data is returned - check 
"ALLOW FILTERING CQL Read Filtered Rows to check what percentage of the data is used"

Alternatives:
- Use a Secondary Index
- Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
spec.title: ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_cql_deletes{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])-sum(rate(scylla_cql_deletes_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

spec.description:
Number of CQL DELETE commands generated by the user

scylla_cql_deletes
spec.title: CQL Deletes by [[by]]

spec.description:
Read requests with ALLOW FILTERING

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side and should be avoided
spec.title: ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads

spec.expr: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_rows_dropped_total{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))  /sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_rows_read_total{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
ALLOW FILTERING Filtered rows, the percentage of rows that were read and then filtered.

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side. 
Reading a row and then filter it is a waste of resources.

Sources: CQL Read requests with "ALLOW FILTERING"

ALLOW FILTERING should be used when large parts of the filtered data is returned

Alternatives:
- Use a Secondary Index
- Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
spec.title: ALLOW FILTERING Filtered Rows

spec.description:
CQL Queries with ALLOW FILTERING should be avoided.
Dropped rows are rows that were read but were filtered by the server.
When dropped rows is relatively high you should consider the alternatives
spec.title: ALLOW FILTERING CQL Read Filtering

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_cql_select_partition_range_scan_no_bypass_cache{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))/(sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) )) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
Range scans should typically by pass the cache.

 Add BYPASS CACHE to your select queries.
spec.title: Range Scans

spec.description:
Range scans should typically by pass the cache.

 Add BYPASS CACHE to your select queries.
spec.title: Range Scans Without BYPASS CACHE

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ANY"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

spec.description: Using consistency level ANY in a query may hurt persistency, if the node receiving the request will fail the data may be lost
spec.title: CQL ANY Queries

spec.description: Using consistency level ANY in a query may hurt persistency, if the node receiving the request will fail the data may be lost
spec.title: CQL ANY CL Queries

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ALL"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

spec.description: Using consistency level ALL in a query may hurt availability, if a node is unavailable operations will fail
spec.title: CQL ALL CL Queries

spec.description: Using consistency level ALL in a query may hurt availability, if a node is unavailable operations will fail
spec.title: CQL ALL CL Queries

spec.description:
Using a multi partition Logged BATCH is considered bad practice.

It does not provide atomicity guarantees, and reduces performance, as a request sent to a random node, not the right replica or shard.
spec.title: Multi partition BATCH

spec.expr: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_cql_updates{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])-sum(rate(scylla_cql_updates_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

spec.description:
Number of CQL UPDATE commands generated by the user

scylla_cql_updates
spec.title: CQL Updates by [[by]]

spec.description:
Using a multi partition Logged BATCH is considered bad practice.

It does not provide atomicity guarantees, and reduces performance, as a request sent to a random node, not the right replica or shard.
spec.title: Multi partition BATCH

options.content:
Cross DC Information

This section is relevant only if you have more than one DC

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ONE"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
How many Queries use Consistency level ONE

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level ONE in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_ONE instead
spec.title: CQL ONE Queries

spec.description:
How many Queries use Consistency level ONE

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level ONE in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_ONE instead
spec.title: CQL ONE CL Queries

spec.expr: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="QUORUM"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
How many Queries use Consistency level QUORUM

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level QUORUM in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_QUORUM instead
spec.title: CQL QUORUM CL Queries

spec.description:
How many Queries use Consistency level QUORUM

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level QUORUM in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_QUORUM instead
spec.title: CQL QUORUM CL Queries

spec.expr: 100*(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", datacenter=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])))/sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

spec.description:
Cross DC traffic may cause additional latencies and network loads and in most cases, should be avoided.

Cross DC Read requests sources:
- Consistency Level that is not LOCAL_XXX
- Tables with read_repair_chance > 0

Note:
- If requests are supposed to be  DC local - verify client is using a DCAware policy and a LOCAL_XX consistency level
spec.title: Cross DC read requests $dc
